title: Text, Hyperlinks, Multimedia & Embedding
summary: Images, links, text emphasis, and native audio/video — how to add rich content students can see and hear.

examples.title: The img element
examples.description: The element displays a picture using the src attribute. Always add alt text so screen readers and search engines understand the image. Change the width or replace the image URL with another https link.

files.html:
Campus photo

alt describes the image if it cannot load.

examples.title: Hyperlinks
examples.description: The <a> element creates a hyperlink using href. Link to other pages, sections on the same page (#id), or external sites. Change the external link to open MDN HTML docs.

files.html:
Navigation links

Jump to tips on this page

Open MDN HTML guide (new tab)

Tips section

You jumped here using an internal anchor link (#tips).

examples.title: Bold vs emphasis: b and em
examples.description: <b> makes text visually bold; <em> adds emphasis and usually appears italic — screen readers treat them differently. Prefer <strong> and <em> for meaning; use <b> for visual bold without extra importance. Change one <b> to <strong> and compare.

files.html:
Visual bold (b): This word is bold for appearance only.

Strong importance: This text is bold because it matters.

Emphasis (em): Stress this phrase when reading aloud.

Compare: bold look vs spoken emphasis.
files.css:
body { font-family: Georgia, serif; padding: 1.5rem; max-width: 34rem; line-height: 1.8; }
p { margin: 0.75rem 0; }

examples.title: The mark element
examples.description: The <mark> element highlights text as if marked with a highlighter pen. Search results, study notes, or key vocabulary. Mark a different word in the sentence.

files.html:
Search result

Students searched for HTML and found this lesson about tags and attributes.

In your own notes, use mark to flag words to review before a test.

examples.title: The video element
examples.description: The <video> element plays video files with built-in browser controls. Tutorials, demos, or recorded lessons — no Flash plugin needed. Change the width or add a poster image attribute.

files.html:
Sample tutorial clip

Text inside <video> shows only if video cannot play.

examples.title: The audio element
examples.description: The <audio> element plays sound files with controls like play and volume. Podcasts, pronunciation guides, or background narration. Add a second audio line with a different src URL.

files.html:
Listen: pronunciation guide

Use controls so users can play, pause, and adjust volume.

practice.description: Create a page with one image (with alt text), one link to https://developer.mozilla.org/, one sentence using <em>, and one <mark> highlight.

starter.html: My media practice

hints: img needs src and alt attributes
Use target="_blank" for external links opened in a new tab
em is for emphasis, mark is for highlighted text
